title: Three- and four-boson systems expanded around the unitarity limit: Application to $^4$He
authors: Feng Wu, Xincheng Lin, Ubirajara van Kolck, Sebastian König
abstract: The three- and four-boson systems with a large scattering length and a short effective range in the two-body sector are studied in the framework of Short-Range Effective Field Theory. The starting point (leading order) of the EFT is taken to be the universal unitarity limit, where the two-body sector is parameter-free and only one three-body parameter enters. In this limit, physical systems manifests discrete scale invariance. Deviations from universality arising from finite scattering-length and effective-range corrections, as well as a four-body force required by renormalization, are included perturbatively at next-to-leading order. The three-body ground state and its associated four-body ground and first-excited states are studied using the Faddeev-Yakubovsky formalism and a complementary diagrammatic approach. By employing techniques to remove contributions from deep trimers in tetramer calculations, we extend our analysis to larger cutoffs than previously accessible within the FY approach. Our results for binding energies and radii of $^4$He three- and four-atom systems converge well to results obtained with sophisticated phenomenological potentials. These successes suggest that the physics of $^4$He atomic clusters is governed by only small deviations from discrete scale invariance.
summary: This paper develops a rigorous Effective Field Theory framework to study three- and four-boson systems near the unitarity limit, applying it to $^4$He. By systematically calculating corrections beyond the leading order, the authors show that the resulting binding energies and radii match established phenomenological results, confirming the underlying physics is robustly described by small deviations from scale invariance.
main_problem: The study investigates three- and four-boson systems near the unitarity limit, applying the theoretical framework to the Helium-4 ($^4$He) system.
main_result: The binding energies and radii of $^4$He three- and four-atom systems calculated using the EFT approach converge well with sophisticated phenomenological potentials, suggesting the physics is governed by small deviations from discrete scale invariance.
method: The authors employ Short-Range Effective Field Theory (SREFT) combined with the Faddeev-Yakubovsky formalism and diagrammatic techniques to systematically calculate corrections beyond the leading-order unitarity limit.
model_or_system: The system consists of three- and four-boson interactions, modeled using an EFT Lagrangian. The calculations are performed by systematically including corrections from finite scattering lengths and effective ranges, necessitating the introduction of four-body forces at next-to-leading order.
key_observables: Binding energies ($B_3, B_4$) and radii ($r_3, r_4$) of the few-boson clusters.
important_parameters: The unitarity limit, the three-body scale parameter ($\Lambda_{\star}$), the two-body scattering length ($a$), and the effective range ($r_2$).
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes that the physics is dominated by small deviations from discrete scale invariance, allowing for a systematic perturbative expansion around the unitarity limit.
figures_summary: Figures compare the tetramer ground-state binding energy and radius against the regulator cutoff ($\Lambda/\kappa_{3,0}$) using different angular momentum cutoffs ($l_{max}$) and theoretical orders (LO/NLO), demonstrating convergence.
paper_structure: The paper systematically builds the theory from the two-body sector (Lippmann-Schwinger) to the three-body (Faddeev) and four-body (FY) equations, detailing the necessary renormalization procedures and the removal of deep trimer states via pseudopotential projection.
why_interesting: This work provides a highly systematic, quantum-field-theoretic approach to few-body physics, which is crucial for understanding ultracold atomic gases tuned near Feshbach resonances (unitarity limit).
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Figure 1. Dressed dimer propagator (double solid line) as a sum of bare dimer propagators (double dotted lines) with insertions of loops with boson propagators (single solid lines).
Figure 2. Diagrammatic representation of the integral equation for the boson-dimer amplitude t3. The bare trimer propagator is represented by a triple dotted line. Other lines as in Fig. 1.
Figure 3. Diagrammatic representation of the four-body integral equations. Lines as in Figs. 1 and 2.

Figure 4. Diagrammatic representation of the four-body in- tegral equations in terms of three-body amplitudes t3 and t′ 3. Both single-boson legs attached to t3 are on-shell, whereas for t′ 3 only the single-boson leg on the side of the (3+1) fragmen- tation is on-shell [35]. Lines as in Figs. 1 and 2.
Figure 5. Contributions of the four-body force g4, represented by a red pentagon, in the diagrammatic four-body integral equation depicted in Fig. 3.
